name: Southeastern Route through the Pinares
name_original: Bosque de Pinares — circular desde Covaleda
description: Cycle 35.4 miles (56.9 km) through the stunning pine forests around Covaleda on this moderate road cycling route with 2901 feet (884 metres)
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Covaleda traverse a landscape characterized by extensive pine forests, mountainous terrain, and glacial formations within the Soria province of Spain. The region offers varied elevation gains and predominantly paved surfaces suitable for road bikes. Cyclists can explore routes leading to significant natural features such as the Picos de Urbión and the Laguna Negra. The area's roads often provide quiet cycling experiences through serene natural environments. Best road cycling routes around Covaleda The most popular road cycling route is Lagunas de Neila from Puerto El Collado — circular through the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Neila, a 41.2 miles (66.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 29 minutes to complete. This difficult route explores the mountainous terrain and glacial lagoons of the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Neila. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Southeastern Route through the Pinares, a moderate 35.4 miles (56.9 km) path. This route winds through the extensive pine forests characteristic of the Covaleda region. Local road cyclists also love the El Royo – Valdeavellano de Tera loop from Museo del Bosque, a 40.9 miles (65.9 km) trail leading through the Soria province landscape, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Covaleda is defined by expansive pine forests, mountainous ascents towards features like the Picos de Urbión, and routes leading to glacial lagoons such as Laguna Negra. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Covaleda are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from 7 reviews. More than 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Covaleda's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Covaleda?
A: Road cycling routes in Covaleda traverse a diverse landscape characterized by extensive pine forests, mountainous terrain, and glacial formations. You'll find varying elevation gains, from moderate climbs to challenging ascents, with predominantly paved surfaces suitable for road bikes. The area's roads often provide quiet cycling experiences through serene natural environments.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Covaleda region?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes available around Covaleda, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from 7 reviews.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easier ride?
A: Yes, Covaleda offers routes for various ability levels. While many routes feature challenging climbs, there are 3 easy routes and 19 moderate routes available. These provide a less strenuous experience while still allowing you to enjoy the region's natural beauty.

Q: What are some of the most scenic natural attractions accessible by road bike?
A: Road cycling routes can lead you to significant natural features such as the Picos de Urbión and the Laguna Negra. The ascent to Laguna Negra, for example, is a demanding ride with sections reaching a 7% gradient. You can also explore routes that wind through expansive pine forests and along the Duero River, offering picturesque scenery. Consider exploring the area around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2516913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Urbión&lt;/a&gt; or the enigmatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6224900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Covaleda offers 15 difficult routes. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1256309113/lagunas-de-neila-from-puerto-el-collado-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-las-lagunas-de-neila" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lagunas de Neila from Puerto El Collado — circular through the Parque Natural de las Lagunas de Neila&lt;/a&gt;, a 66.3 km trail with over 1200 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40305450" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Neila Lagoons – Direction Puerto El Collado loop from Museo del Bosque&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 116.6 km with over 1800 meters of climbing.

Q: What kind of views can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: The mountainous and forested environment of Covaleda provides continuous ascents and descents, offering dynamic road cycling experiences. You can expect quiet roads with views of valleys, distant peaks, and the expansive pine forests that characterize the region. The area's altitude variations ensure a variety of gradients and stunning vistas.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Covaleda?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes around Covaleda are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38248965" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Royo – Valdeavellano de Tera loop from Museo del Bosque&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 65.9 km circular route through the Soria province landscape.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Covaleda?
A: The region offers beautiful cycling experiences throughout much of the year. While summer provides warm weather, autumn is particularly appealing when the extensive pine forests transform into vibrant colors, offering visually stunning rides. The serene natural environment is enjoyable in most seasons, but always check local weather conditions before heading out.

Q: Are there any specific points of interest or landmarks near the cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, you can find several points of interest. These include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt;, the charming settlement of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721568" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vinuesa and the Cuerda del Pozo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the intriguing &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2721569" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Submerged Roman Bridge of Vinuesa&lt;/a&gt;. These can be great additions to your cycling itinerary.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Covaleda?
A: The road cycling routes in Covaleda are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an impressive average rating of 4.9 stars from 7 reviews. Over 900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Covaleda's varied terrain, often praising the quiet roads, challenging ascents, and the immersive experience of cycling through the vast pine forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: The Covaleda region is known for its numerous waterfalls, such as those along the Route of the Covaleda Waterfalls, including Paso Peñoncito Waterfall and Ojeda River Waterfall. While some waterfalls might require a short hike to reach, the scenic routes leading to their trailheads contribute to a diverse outdoor experience and can be incorporated into your ride.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Picos de Urbión?
A: Yes, Covaleda is located at the foothills of the Sierra de Urbión, and there are paved roads that lead into the stunning pine forests and offer routes with substantial climbs and descents, providing views towards the Picos de Urbión. The ascent to Urbión from Covaleda is part of the GR-86 Soriano Iberian Trail, showcasing beautiful pine forests and offering panoramic views from higher elevations.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Bosque de Pinares — circular desde Covaleda road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route is approximately 56.9 kilometers (35.4 miles) long. Cyclists with a good level of fitness can expect to complete it in about 3 hours and 2 minutes, depending on their pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the starting point for this circular road cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Covaleda, Soria. You can begin your ride from any convenient point within the town, as it loops back to its origin.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for road cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is characterized by mostly well-paved surfaces, making it generally smooth and enjoyable for road cyclists. You'll be cycling through the scenic pine forests, with some ascents and descents due to the region's irregular topography.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Bosque de Pinares circular route?
faqs.answer: As the name suggests, the route offers deep immersion in the magnificent pine forests, particularly the Pinar Grande de Covaleda, one of Europe's largest Scots pine forests. You'll also pass by the mountain pass known as El Cargadero, and experience the natural beauty of the Pine Forest, mushroom trails area, which is rich in natural resources and varied landscapes.

faqs.question: Does this road cycling route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other paths. You'll find yourself on parts of the Ruta del Sureste, [CIMA SO02] El Cargadero, Ruta Brabojo, and Ruta La Morra del Fraile, among others. This offers a chance to experience different facets of the local trail network.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque de Pinares — circular desde Covaleda route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate. While the terrain is mostly well-paved, it covers nearly 57 kilometers with an elevation gain of about 880 meters. A good level of fitness is recommended to comfortably tackle the ascents and the overall distance. It might be challenging for absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The pine forests and well-maintained roads make this route enjoyable across several seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can also be good, but be mindful of potential heat, especially during midday. Winter might bring colder temperatures and possibly snow at higher elevations, which could affect road conditions.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the Comarca de Pinares area?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for road cycling on public roads within the Comarca de Pinares or for this particular route. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning nature conservation and private property. If you plan to explore off-road trails or specific natural parks, it's always good to check for any local rules.

faqs.question: Is the Bosque de Pinares — circular desde Covaleda route dog-friendly for cyclists?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route on public roads. While dogs are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a dog attached to your bike can be unsafe for both you and your pet, especially on a route with moderate difficulty and traffic. If you wish to bring your dog, consider alternative activities like hiking on designated trails where dogs are permitted, usually on a leash.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or restaurants along the route, or should I pack provisions?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting and ending in Covaleda, you'll find amenities like cafes and restaurants in the town itself. However, the route primarily traverses natural pine forest areas, so there are limited services directly along the 57-kilometer stretch. It's advisable to pack sufficient water and snacks for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car when starting this route in Covaleda?
faqs.answer: Covaleda, being the starting and ending point, offers various public parking options within the town. You should be able to find suitable parking near the town center or residential areas to begin your ride conveniently.
